prompt: Design a HarmonyHub Multi-Tenant Performing Arts & Concessions Portal to let organizations run offline-ready casting directories and event meal fulfillment from a single, secure site. The Vue.js web interface serves four roles: Student (performs and orders), Referee (event support staff with limited ordering and roster visibility), Staff (program and kitchen operators), and Administrator (tenant owner). Users sign in with a local username and password, land on an event dashboard, and can search and filter the talent directory by actor, repertoire, tags (e.g., "jazz," "monologue"), region, and availability windows; result cards show masked contact details until the user has permission to view them. The portal offers configurable recommendations on the directory and repertoire library (popularity over the last 30 days, recent activity in the last 72 hours, and tag matching), with Staff able to pin featured entries and manage allowlists/blocklists to prevent inappropriate pairings. For event operations, users can place concession meal orders for pickup or delivery, maintain an address book with US-style addresses, select 15-minute time slots, and see an estimated meal-ready time that updates as the kitchen workload changes; pickup uses a rotating 6-digit code shown in the order screen and verified at handoff. Staff configure delivery zones with flat fees (for example, $0.00 pickup, $3.99 within Zone A, $6.99 within Zone B) and set slot capacity (for example, 40 meals per 15-minute slot); if capacity is exceeded, the UI blocks checkout and suggests the next available slot. Staff can bulk import members and rosters from CSV, merge duplicates, and freeze accounts for policy violations, with clear in-app status feedback and undo where permitted. The backend uses FastAPI to expose REST-style endpoints over site-wide HTTPS (self-signed certificates supported for on-prem use) and enforces tenant isolation across organization/project/store contexts with RBAC and optional ABAC rules for menu visibility, API access, and row-/column-level data scopes tied to department–grade–class dimensions. PostgreSQL persists all data locally, using a hybrid relational model plus JSONB documents for flexible profiles, tags, and recommendation signals; ingestion is layered so raw imports are stored unchanged, then normalized into processed tables, and indexed/partitioned by repertoire item (including song metadata where applicable), time, and user to keep search responsive under bulk loads. Security controls include salted password hashing, CSRF/XSS/SQL-injection defenses, strict input validation, anti-replay nonces with a 5-minute timestamp window, and API rate limits (default 60 requests/minute per user, 300/minute per device IP). Brute-force protection locks accounts for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, with optional offline MFA via TOTP. Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest and masked in logs and UI; exports require explicit permission and are recorded. Operation auditing captures logins, permission changes, imports/exports, order fulfillment steps, and admin actions, retained for 12 months, with nightly local backups and quarterly recovery drills to a designated offline medium. Secure file upload accepts only approved types (CSV, PDF, JPG/PNG) with size limits (25 MB), magic-byte validation, and SHA-256 fingerprinting to detect tampering without relying on any external scanning services.
